alerts.title: Iran launches second ballistic missile salvo at Israel; ceasefire unraveling
alerts.summary: Iranian forces fired at least 12 additional ballistic missiles at northern Israel in a second wave within 15 minutes of the first strike, according to Israeli media reports and Rybar Telegram analysis. Launches originated from Tabriz and Isfahan; Israeli air defenses reported active interception over Haifa. This marks sustained escalation following Israeli strikes on Beirut's Hezbollah headquarters (Ad-Dakhieh) that killed civilians, signaling ceasefire collapse and potential entry into direct Iran-Israel kinetic war phase.

alerts.title: Iran nuclear enrichment at 60% U-235; breakout window narrows to 1-2 weeks
alerts.summary: US intelligence and IAEA reporting confirm Iranian uranium enrichment has reached 60% U-235 purity with estimated breakout time of 1-2 weeks, per multiple sources in global_metrics nuclear headlines. Simultaneous US draft IAEA resolution demands Iran account for bombed nuclear sites and uranium stocks. Escalation in kinetic theater combined with accelerated nuclear timeline creates compounding strategic crisis.

alerts.title: US sentiment collapses to 64% negative amid Iran escalation and Trump diplomacy debate
alerts.summary: Negative sentiment in US news spiked to 64% (up from baseline) with Trump dominating trending terms (67 mentions). Word frequencies show 'clash,' 'deal,' and 'drone' rising sharply alongside Iran and Israel mentions, reflecting public anxiety over escalation control and Trump's de-escalation messaging versus military realities. Sentiment swing indicates domestic political fracture over conflict posture.

alerts.title: Israel strikes Beirut's Ad-Dakhieh Hezbollah center despite Trump restraint appeal
alerts.summary: Israeli warplanes struck Hezbollah's historic headquarters in southern Beirut suburb using US-supplied GBU precision munitions, killing at least 2 civilians, per Al Jazeera and New York Times. Strike occurred after Trump's explicit public statement urging Netanyahu 'not to strike back' and calling Iran missile response 'enough.' Israeli defiance of Trump's restraint call signals Netanyahu's rejection of de-escalation and direct challenge to US diplomatic authority.

alerts.title: Threat level jumps to 9/10 CRITICAL; three carrier strike groups maintain forward posture amid dual escalation
alerts.summary: Global threat meter escalated to 9/10 (CRITICAL—IMMINENT ESCALATION) per global_metrics. USS Dwight D. Eisenhower, USS Abraham Lincoln, and USS Gerald R. Ford remain deployed across Red Sea, Arabian Sea, and Mediterranean; USS Florida submarine positioned in Persian Gulf. Brent crude spiked to $97.35/bbl; Iranian rial weakened to 1.37M/USD. Proxy status elevated: Hezbollah active, Houthis conducting multiple recent attacks, Iraqi Shiite militias elevated with detected base strikes.

analysis_note: US-Iran ceasefire framework has collapsed in real-time as Israel defied Trump's de-escalation calls and Iran responded with sustained ballistic barrages; nuclear breakout window narrowing to weeks while three US carrier groups maintain battle stations and Trump attempts diplomatic override of military momentum.
